 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-67r","title":"P4.2 Source badge/tag in result rows","description":"# P4.2 Source badge/tag in result rows

## Overview
Add a compact badge or tag showing the source hostname for each result row,
providing at-a-glance source identification.

## Implementation Details

### Badge Format
- Local sessions: `[local]` or just omit badge
- Remote sessions: `[hostname]` abbreviated if needed (e.g., `[laptop]`, `[work-pc]`)
- Badge appears after agent name or in dedicated column

### Abbreviation Logic
```rust
fn abbreviate_hostname(hostname: &str, max_len: usize) -> String {
    if hostname.len() <= max_len {
        hostname.to_string()
    } else {
        // Take first part before dots, truncate if needed
        let first_part = hostname.split('.').next().unwrap_or(hostname);
        if first_part.len() <= max_len {
            first_part.to_string()
        } else {
            format!("{}…", &first_part[..max_len-1])
        }
    }
}
```

### Row Layout Update
```
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ claude-code [laptop]  /projects/myapp  "Fixed auth"  0.95 │
│ cursor      [local]   /projects/other  "API refactor" 0.87│
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Badge Styling
```rust
let badge_style = match source_type {
    SourceType::Remote => Style::new().fg(Color::Cyan).add_modifier(Modifier::DIM),
    SourceType::Local => Style::new().fg(Color::DarkGray),
};
```

## Dependencies
- Requires P3.3 (SearchHit has source_hostname)
- Can be done in parallel with P4.1

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Badge visible for all results with provenance
- [ ] Hostname abbreviated appropriately (max ~10 chars)
- [ ] Badge doesn't crowd other columns
- [ ] Consistent styling with overall TUI theme","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T06:04:47.272673Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T19:12:06.320665Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T19:12:06.320665Z","close_reason":"Implemented as part of P4.1: Source badge [hostname] added to location line for remote sessions. Uses purple styling with italic modifier.  {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-69y","title":"DOC.1: README Sources System Section","description":"# Task: Document Sources System in README

## Context
The sources system (P1-P6) enables cass to aggregate sessions from multiple machines via SSH/rsync. This is a major differentiating feature that is currently **completely undocumented** in the README.

## What to Document

### Remote Sources Overview
- What: Sync agent sessions from other machines into unified search
- Why: Developers work across laptop/desktop/server - need unified search
- How: SSH/rsync-based sync with provenance tracking

### Configuration
- Location: `~/.config/cass/sources.toml`
- Format: TOML with `[[sources]]` sections
- Fields: name, type, host, paths, sync_schedule
- Example configuration block

### CLI Commands
Document the `cass sources` subcommand family:
- `cass sources list [--verbose] [--json]`
- `cass sources add <url> [--name] [--preset] [--path] [--no-test]`
- `cass sources remove <name> [--purge] [-y]`
- `cass sources doctor [--source] [--json]`
- `cass sources sync [--source] [--no-index] [--verbose] [--dry-run] [--json]`

### Path Mappings (P6.x)
- Purpose: Rewrite remote paths to local equivalents
- Use case: Remote path `/home/dev/project` → local `/Users/me/project`
- Commands:
  - `cass sources mappings list <source> [--json]`
  - `cass sources mappings add <source> --from <remote> --to <local> [--agents]`
  - `cass sources mappings remove <source> <index>`
  - `cass sources mappings test <source> <path> [--agent]`

### Provenance Tracking
- Each conversation tracks origin (local vs remote)
- source_id, source_kind, workspace_original fields
- Used for deduplication and filtering

### TUI Integration
- F11: Cycle source filter (all → local → remote)
- Shift+F11: Source filter menu
- Visual indicators for remote sessions

## Placement in README
Add new section after "Supported Agents" section, titled "Remote Sources" or "Multi-Machine Search".

## Technical Notes
- See `src/sources/mod.rs` for module documentation
- See `src/sources/config.rs` for configuration schema
- See `tests/e2e_sources.rs` for CLI usage examples","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:56:57.213390Z","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:15:56.150777Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T23:15:56.150777Z","close_reason":"Added Remote Sources section to README with full documentation of sources system, path mappings, CLI commands, TUI integration, and provenance tracking","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-69y","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-h2i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:54Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-6afmg","title":"[LOW] health p50 latency fixture can pass without seeded large rows","description":"Session-wide review finding from 91985347/f3dcce6d: tests/e2e_health.rs::health_json_large_seeded_db_p50_stays_under_50ms now intentionally skips DB COUNT(*) in cass health, but the test only references LARGE_HEALTH_DB_CONVERSATIONS/LARGE_HEALTH_DB_MESSAGES via let _ = (...). If seed_large_health_latency_db stops inserting the large row set, the test can still pass against a tiny existing DB and no longer proves the documented large-corpus health fast-surface budget. Fix by asserting the seeded conversations/messages table counts before the timing loop.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T22:22:35.785056675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T22:27:34.018325619Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T22:27:34.017852894Z","close_reason":"Fixed: health p50 fixture now asserts seeded conversation/message row counts before timing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-6bbig","title":"Phase 4D: Charts and data visualization for analytics","description":"Leverage ftui-extras/charts and canvas modules to add data visualization to cass. Add: (1) Session activity heatmap -- a calendar-style heatmap showing coding session frequency per day (like GitHub contribution graph but in the terminal), accessible via command palette or dedicated Analytics view, (2) Agent usage pie/bar chart -- visual breakdown of sessions by agent (Claude vs Codex vs Gemini etc), (3) Token usage sparkline in detail view -- show token consumption over the conversation using Sparkline with gradient colors (green=low, yellow=medium, red=high), (4) Search frequency timeline -- mini chart in footer showing queries per minute, (5) Indexing performance chart -- throughput over time during reindexing. These visualizations make cass uniquely powerful for understanding coding agent usage patterns.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:20:15.709981Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:32.435427Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:57:32.435399Z","close_reason":"Content preserved in new 2noh9.4 bead: Charts + data visualization.  {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-6ecg5","title":"PRE-REQ: Write pre-migration tests for 7 untested connectors","description":"WHAT: Write pre-migration integration tests for the 7 connectors that currently have NO tests: chatgpt, cursor, copilot, factory, openclaw, vibe, clawdbot.

WHY: Before migrating connectors to FAD, we need a baseline test suite to ensure the migration doesn't break anything. Connectors without tests are migration risks -- we'd have no way to verify they still work after moving.

UNTESTED CONNECTORS (7 of 15):
1. chatgpt.rs -- ChatGPT conversation exports (JSON)
2. cursor.rs -- Cursor IDE agent sessions
3. copilot.rs -- GitHub Copilot chat sessions
4. factory.rs -- Factory agent sessions
5. openclaw.rs -- OpenClaw agent sessions
6. vibe.rs -- Vibe coding agent (~150 lines, simple)
7. clawdbot.rs -- Clawdbot agent (~150 lines, simple)

TESTED CONNECTORS (8 of 15, for reference):
- claude_code.rs, codex.rs, aider.rs, cline.rs, amp.rs, gemini.rs, opencode.rs, pi_agent.rs

TEST STRATEGY FOR EACH:
1. Create fixture files in tests/fixtures/{connector_name}/ with realistic but minimal sample data
2. Write detect() test: verify detection logic works for expected directory structures
3. Write scan() test: verify conversation parsing produces correct NormalizedConversation structs
4. Test edge cases: empty files, malformed JSON, missing fields, timestamps at boundaries
5. Test incremental scanning (since_ts parameter)

FIXTURE REQUIREMENTS:
- Fixtures should be small but representative (real format, minimal size)
- Include at least: 1 normal conversation, 1 edge case (empty/malformed)
- Fixtures go in tests/fixtures/ alongside existing ones

TEST TEMPLATE FOR EACH CONNECTOR:
  #[test] fn detect_with_valid_structure()
  #[test] fn detect_with_missing_directory()
  #[test] fn scan_basic_conversation()
  #[test] fn scan_handles_empty_file()
  #[test] fn scan_handles_malformed_json()
  #[test] fn scan_incremental_since_ts()
  #[test] fn scan_preserves_message_ordering()

ORDERING NOTE: vibe and clawdbot are simplest (~150 lines each, no PathTrie) -- start there. chatgpt and cursor are most complex -- do those last.

THIS MUST BE DONE BEFORE ANY CONNECTOR MIGRATION (FAD tasks 5-19).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All 7 connectors have at least 4 tests each (detect valid, detect missing, scan basic, scan edge case)
- All tests pass on current cass codebase
- Fixtures committed to tests/fixtures/
- Tests serve as regression suite for migration verification","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-19T19:40:38.905480Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T21:02:55.627781Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T21:02:55.627759Z","close_reason":"Already completed by another agent. All 7 connectors (chatgpt:13, cursor:13, copilot:9, factory:13, openclaw:14, vibe:9, clawdbot:9 = 80 tests total) have comprehensive test suites that all pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fad","pre-migration","testing"]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-6f118","title":"[MEDIUM] chatgpt connector lacks encrypted v2/v3 success and failure regression coverage","description":"Current repo coverage only exercises the no-key skip path in tests/connector_chatgpt.rs:393-419. The actual parser has unpinned branches for decrypt_file() and encrypted parse/decode failure handling in franken_agent_detection/src/connectors/chatgpt.rs:252-317.

Gap:
- no end-to-end test proving a real encrypted conversation decrypts with a valid key
- no regression for wrong-key / invalid UTF-8 / malformed decrypted JSON behavior

Risk:
- encrypted ChatGPT desktop history can silently stop indexing or regress without any test failure

Suggested fix:
- add a deterministic encrypted fixture under tests/fixtures/chatgpt_real/ plus a test that loads a known key and proves successful v2/v3 decryption
- add a companion regression for wrong-key or malformed decrypted payload handling so failure behavior stays intentional","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:30:05.655959270Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:38:55.429534952Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:38:55.429135394Z","close_reason":"Added encrypted ChatGPT success/failure regression coverage with deterministic encrypted fixtures and oversized-file guard coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
